summary: Linked `src/melee/if/ifnametag.c` by moving it from NonMatching to Matching and making a small source rewrite that brought `un_802FD4C8` to 100% match. The PR also corrected/renamed nametag-related symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, mostly converting previously global unknown objects and literals to local symbols or meaningful local names such as `nametag_eyepos`, `nametag_interest`, and `nametag_CObjDesc`. The decomp.dev report recorded one new match for `main/melee/if/ifnametag`, +316 matched code bytes, +3152 linked code bytes, and +429 linked data bytes.

key_files.role: Source changes to make `un_802FD4C8` match, including an inline GObj creation helper and temporary-variable reshaping.
Updated local/global scope and names for nametag text/data/bss/sdata/sdata2 symbols used by the linked object.

what_changed: `un_802FD4C8` in `src/melee/if/ifnametag.c` was rewritten enough to match: `GObj_Create` was routed through a small inline helper `un_802FD4C8_inline`, the camera object result was stored in a separate `HSD_CObj* new_var`, and the `memzero` size expression was folded through assignment to `i`.
`NameTag_RenderCallback` in `symbols.txt` was marked `scope:local` instead of having no explicit scope.

Several nametag camera descriptor/data labels were renamed from generic `un_`/`lbl_` names to local or compiler-style names, including `nametag_eyepos`, `nametag_interest`, `nametag_CObjDesc`, `@197`, `@378`, and `@379`.
Local section anchor labels were added for nametag-adjacent data and bss regions: `...data.0` at `0x803F98B8` and `...bss.0` at `0x804A1ED0`.
A group of nametag-related string and floating/double constants in `.sdata` and `.sdata2` was converted from `un_804D...` global names to local compiler-style `@NNN` labels.
smart_moves: Used an `inline` wrapper around `GObj_Create(0xE, arg0, 0)` to influence codegen without changing observable behavior.

Introduced an explicit `HSD_CObj* new_var` for the `lb_80013B14((HSD_CameraDescPerspective*) &nametag_CObjDesc)` result before passing it to `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70`, matching the compiler's temporary/register behavior.
Preserved the loop variable `i` by assigning `sizeof(un_804D6D70)` into it inside the `memzero` call, a source-shaping tactic likely needed for instruction/register matching.
Cleaned the symbol map at the same time as linking, reducing global `un_` noise by marking local literals/constants and naming identifiable nametag camera data.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a file to `Matching`, update both the build configuration and the symbol map; linked data scope/names can be as important as the C source match.
Small source-level reshaping, such as inline helper functions and explicit temporaries, can be sufficient to finish a stubborn function without broader refactors.
For decomp symbol hygiene, compiler-generated literals and constants should generally be local `@NNN` labels when there is no stronger semantic name.

Meaningful names are appropriate for identifiable structured data such as nametag camera descriptors or vectors, but evidence should drive the naming; here `nametag_eyepos`, `nametag_interest`, and `nametag_CObjDesc` are grounded by their use in `ifnametag.c`.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem-prefixed semantic names for identifiable nametag data: `nametag_eyepos`, `nametag_interest`, `nametag_CObjDesc`.
Use compiler-style local `@NNN` names for unnamed literals/constants instead of exporting them as `un_804D...` globals.
Use section-local anchor labels like `...data.0` and `...bss.0` where needed for local section references.
Keep function names unchanged when no better semantic name is established; this PR still leaves names like `un_802FD4C8` and `fn_802FCAC4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline wrapper tactic: replace a direct `GObj_Create(HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_UI, 15, 0)` source call with `un_802FD4C8_inline(15)` returning `GObj_Create(0xE, arg0, 0)`.
Temporary variable tactic: store `lb_80013B14(...)` into `HSD_CObj* new_var` before object attachment.
Expression-shaping tactic: call `memzero(un_804D6D70, i = sizeof(un_804D6D70))` rather than passing `sizeof(un_804D6D70)` directly.
Symbol-scope tactic: mark data, bss, sdata, and sdata2 entries as `scope:local` when they are local literals/constants needed for matching linked output.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report showed one new match: `main/melee/if/ifnametag` item `un_802FD4C8` improved from 90.18% to 100.00%, adding +32 bytes for that item.
Overall GALE01 metrics improved: matched code 61.59% (+0.01%, +316 bytes), linked code 33.82% (+0.08%, +3152 bytes), linked data 32.36% (+0.04%, +429 bytes).

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that used inline helper functions to force matching around `GObj_Create` or other HSD object constructors.
Search for remaining `melee/if` files still marked `NonMatching` in `configure.py`.
Audit remaining `un_` globals near `ifnametag` data/sdata2 ranges to see whether they should be local `@NNN` labels or semantic nametag names.
Look for other nametag functions near `fn_802FCAC4`, `un_802FCBA0`, and `fn_802FCC44` that may become matchable after this symbol cleanup.
